request.system:
You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.

Direction: judge whether this event makes the contract MORE likely to resolve YES (= bullish), LESS likely to resolve YES (= bearish), or has no directional effect (= neutral). IMPORTANT: direction is always relative to YES resolution. If your one_line_why says the event 'increases odds' or 'raises probability', direction MUST be bullish. If it says 'decreases odds' or 'lowers probability', direction MUST be bearish. Do not contradict yourself.

Magnitude: high = materially changes probability, medium = notable but not decisive, low = incremental signal.

Timeline shift: does this event suggest resolution will come sooner or later than expected, or no change (none)?
request.user:
Contract title: Will the SEC approve a spot Bitcoin ETF by January 15, 2024?
Resolution criteria: Resolves YES if any spot Bitcoin ETF receives approval from the U.S. Securities
and Exchange Commission by January 15, 2024, 11:59 PM ET. Approval means an
effective SEC order permitting listing on NYSE Arca, Cboe BZX, or NASDAQ.
Resolves NO otherwise.

Conditions:
  A: SEC OKs NYSE Arca spot BTC ETF — An effective SEC order by Jan 15, 2024, 11:59 PM ET permits listing of any spot Bitcoin ETF on NYSE Arca.
  B: SEC OKs Cboe BZX spot BTC ETF — An effective SEC order by Jan 15, 2024, 11:59 PM ET permits listing of any spot Bitcoin ETF on Cboe BZX.
  C: SEC OKs Nasdaq spot BTC ETF — An effective SEC order by Jan 15, 2024, 11:59 PM ET permits listing of any spot Bitcoin ETF on NASDAQ.
Settlement entities: ['U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission', 'Division of Trading and Markets', 'Division of Investment Management', 'BlackRock', 'Fidelity Investments', 'VanEck', 'Grayscale Investments', 'Ark Investment Management', 'Invesco', 'WisdomTree']

Record title: La vigilancia al trabajador amenaza la privacidad
Record date: 2023-10-30
Record regulator: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
Record topic: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
Record entities: ['ACLU', 'Brookings Institution', 'CFPB', 'Commodity Futures Trading Commission', 'Department of Justice', 'EEOC', 'Federal Trade Commission', 'Harvard Business Review', 'National Credit Union Administration', 'New Hampshire Union Leader', 'Office of Director of National Intelligence', 'Office of Science and Technology Policy', 'RAND Corporation', 'Securities and Exchange Commission', 'The White House', 'UC San Francisco', 'Washington Post', 'White House OSTP']

response.one_line_why: CFPB report on worker surveillance/privacy is unrelated to SEC decisions on spot Bitcoin ETF approvals, so it doesn't change odds.
